name: Creamy Chicken Popovers
recipeIngredient: 1/4 Cup Dry White Wine or Chablis
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine water, chicken, carrot, and sliced onion in a large saucepan.
Bring to a boil; cover, reduce heat, and simmer for 30 minutes.
Remove the chicken from the broth and cool it down.
2. Strain the broth through a fine-mesh sieve into a bowl, reserving 3/4 cup of the broth.
3. Coat a large nonstick skillet with cooking spray and place over medium-high heat until hot.
Add the chopped onion and sauté until tender. Gradually add 1/4 cup flour
and tarragon, stirring well. Gradually stir in 3/4 cup skim milk,
yogurt, wine, and reserved 3/4 cup chicken broth. Cook, stirring constantly,
until the mixture thickens and bubbles.
4. Stir in peas, pimento, and the reserved chicken pieces.
Set aside and keep warm.
5. In a separate bowl, combine the remaining 1 cup flour and salt,
stirring well. Add eggs and the remaining 1 cup skim milk;
beat with a wire whisk until well blended.
6. Spoon equal amounts of the egg mixture into six 6-ounce custard cups coated with cooking spray.
Place them about 4 inches apart on a baking sheet.
Bake at 400 degrees F for 45-50 minutes or until golden brown.
7. Carefully spoon half of the chicken mixture over each popover.
Serve immediately.
